name: mdBook
description: Rust-powered tool for creating books and documentation from Markdown files — used by the Rust Book, mdBook, and many open source projects
when_to_use: Creating structured documentation sites, technical books, and project guides from Markdown with search and theming

install.command: brew install mdbook || cargo install mdbook

name: Hugo
description: Fastest static site generator — builds documentation sites, blogs, and landing pages from Markdown and Go templates in milliseconds
when_to_use: Documentation sites, project websites, and blogs that need fast build times and rich theming with zero JavaScript dependencies

install.command: brew install hugo || go install github.com/gohugoio/hugo@latest

name: Docusaurus
description: React-based documentation framework by Meta — Markdown docs with versioning, i18n, search, and a plugin ecosystem
when_to_use: Documentation sites needing versioning, internationalization, blog integration, and React component embedding

name: Asciidoctor
description: AsciiDoc processor and publishing toolchain — converts AsciiDoc to HTML, PDF, EPUB, and man pages with rich formatting beyond Markdown
when_to_use: Technical documentation requiring tables, admonitions, cross-references, and multi-format output that Markdown cannot handle well

install.command: brew install asciidoctor || gem install asciidoctor

name: Pandoc
description: Universal document converter — converts between Markdown, HTML, LaTeX, DOCX, PDF, EPUB, reStructuredText, and dozens more formats
when_to_use: Converting between document formats, generating PDFs from Markdown, creating DOCX reports from templates, multi-format publishing

install.command: brew install pandoc || sudo apt install pandoc
